implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: create_pr in src/forge/adapters/gh/operations.py returning PRResult
AC-002: Pre-flight GH_TOKEN check returns missing_credentials without invoking gh
AC-003: gh pr create with --title/--body/--base/--draft via _execute with cwd=worktree
AC-004: PR URL regex parsing populates pr_url and pr_number from trailing slash
AC-005: Non-zero exit returns PRResult(status=failed, stderr=...) without exception
AC-006: try/except Exception wrapper guarantees never-raises (ADR-ARCH-025)
AC-007: List-token argv passes shell metacharacters literally
AC-008: 17 unit tests covering all five required scenarios; code passes Black/PEP 8 conventions

completion_promises.evidence: create_pr is implemented in src/forge/adapters/gh/operations.py with signature (worktree: Path, title: str, body: str, base: str = 'main', draft: bool = False) -> PRResult, exported via src/forge/adapters/gh/__init__.py. Verified by TestCreatePrReturnContract (4 tests) which check isinstance(result, PRResult) on every code path (missing creds, success, failure, exception). The BDD key-example oracle also asserts isinstance(result, PRResult) on the happy path.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Pre-flight check: if GH_TOKEN is unset (or empty) in os.environ, return PRResult(status='failed', error_code='missing_credentials', stderr='GH_TOKEN not set in environment') without invoking gh
completion_promises.evidence: create_pr() reads os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN', '') on every call (no import-time caching) and returns _missing_credentials_result() when the value is falsy. Verified by TestCreatePrMissingCredentials::test_missing_gh_token_returns_missing_credentials_failure (asserts execute_mock.assert_not_called()), test_empty_gh_token_is_treated_as_missing (asserts the same for empty-string), and test_gh_token_is_re_read_on_every_call (toggles env between calls). BDD scenario test_negative_create_pr_missing_credentials passes against the real adapter via the pytest-bdd oracle.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Otherwise, invoke gh pr create --title <t> --body <b> --base <base> (plus --draft if requested) via DeepAgents execute with cwd = worktree
completion_promises.evidence: _build_command() assembles ['gh', 'pr', 'create', '--title', title, '--body', body, '--base', base] with optional '--draft' append, and create_pr passes cwd=str(worktree) to _execute. Verified by test_default_command_shape_uses_separate_tokens (exact list match), test_draft_flag_appends_dash_dash_draft (--draft & --base develop override), and BDD test_key_example_create_pr_success which asserts cwd == str(worktree) and the canonical argv shape.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On success, parse the PR URL from gh's stdout and populate PRResult.pr_url and PRResult.pr_number (parsed from the trailing slash component of the URL)
completion_promises.evidence: Module-level _PR_URL_PATTERN regex r'https://github\.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/pull/(\d+)' is searched against stdout.strip(); match.group(0) becomes pr_url and int(match.group(1)) becomes pr_number. Verified by test_success_parses_pr_url_and_number_from_stdout (expects pr_number=123) and test_pr_number_parsed_from_trailing_slash_component (multiline stdout, expects pr_number=4567). BDD scenario asserts pr_number=77.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On non-zero exit, PRResult(status='failed', stderr=...), no exception
completion_promises.evidence: When exit_code != 0, create_pr returns PRResult(status='failed', stderr=stderr.strip() or stdout.strip() or None) without raising. error_code remains None (only credential failures populate it). Verified by test_non_zero_exit_returns_failed_with_stderr which asserts 'not a git repository' in result.stderr and result.error_code is None.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Function body wrapped in try/except Exception as exc: returning a failed PRResult — never raises (ADR-ARCH-025)
completion_promises.evidence: The _execute call is wrapped in try/except Exception, converting exc to PRResult(status='failed', stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}'). Verified by test_execute_exception_is_caught_and_returned_as_failed (RuntimeError) and test_return_type_is_prresult_on_exception (ValueError). Also catches the 0-exit-no-URL contract violation as 'failed' to avoid fabricating success.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Arguments containing shell metacharacters (e.g. backticks in PR body) are passed as separate list tokens to execute, never via shell-string concatenation
completion_promises.evidence: _build_command() builds a Python list and passes each user value as its own argv element; _execute uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*command) (no shell=True, no string concatenation). Verified by test_backticks_in_body_pass_through_literal ('Run `rm -rf /` to test'), test_dollar_signs_in_body_pass_through_literal ('Set $HOME and $PATH explicitly'), and test_metacharacters_in_title_pass_through_literal ('Fix `bug` in $service && rm tmp'). Each asserts the literal string is the next list element after --body or --title.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests: missing GH_TOKEN, success path with parsed URL, non-zero exit, raised exception, body containing backticks/dollar-signs; All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/adapters/gh/test_operations.py contains 17 tests organised into 5 test classes mirroring the AC list: TestCreatePrMissingCredentials (3), TestCreatePrSuccessPath (4), TestCreatePrFailurePath (3), TestCreatePrShellMetacharacters (3), TestCreatePrReturnContract (4). All 17 unit tests + 2 BDD oracle scenarios pass. Code uses type hints, snake_case, f-strings, list-token argv, max line under 88, follows the project's PEP 8 / Black conventions documented in .claude/rules/code-style.md.
